chapter: The Mediterranean Diet: Where Taste Meets Health

claims.claim: Provides monounsaturated fatty acids that reduce LDL-cholesterol and heart disease risk
claims.mechanism: Replaces saturated fat intake; monounsaturated fat substitution for 5% of saturated fat calories associated with 15% lower coronary heart disease risk

claims.claim: Contains oleocanthal which inhibits inflammation
claims.mechanism: Oleocanthal phytochemical inhibits inflammatory pathways, similar mechanism to ibuprofen

claims.claim: Reduces circulating oxidised LDL and inflammatory markers
claims.mechanism: Antioxidant compounds prevent oxidation of LDL particles, reducing binding to endothelial and immune cell receptors that drive atherosclerotic plaque growth

claims.claim: Mediterranean diet supplemented with EVOO reduces cardiovascular events, especially strokes
claims.mechanism: Combined anti-inflammatory, cholesterol-lowering and antioxidant effects reduce atherosclerosis progression

claims.claim: May reduce risk of type 2 diabetes, peripheral artery disease, atrial fibrillation and breast cancer
claims.mechanism: Multiple pathways including reduced inflammation and oxidative stress

consumption.amount: About 25% of daily calories from olive oil (as part of Mediterranean diet)
consumption.preparation: Used as dressing, not for high-heat cooking based on traditional use described
consumption.reasoning: Keys found ~25% of calories in traditional Neapolitan diet came from EVOO and wine combined

relationships.description: Both provide monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fats; PREDIMED trial tested both EVOO-supplemented and nut-supplemented Mediterranean diets with similar cardiovascular benefits
Both contain anti-inflammatory phytochemicals (oleocanthal in EVOO, ferulic acid in whole grains) that work through complementary pathways
EVOO enhances absorption of fat-soluble antioxidants (beta-carotene, vitamin E) from vegetables

claims.claim: Five servings per week associated with 40-60% reduction in heart attack risk
claims.mechanism: Provide polyunsaturated omega-3 fatty acids (walnuts) and omega-6 fatty acids plus plant sterols (almonds, hazelnuts, pine nuts) that reduce LDL-cholesterol

claims.claim: Plant sterols reduce LDL-cholesterol levels
claims.mechanism: Plant sterols compete with cholesterol for absorption in the intestine, reducing circulating LDL-cholesterol

claims.claim: Part of cholesterol-lowering food portfolio that lowers LDL-cholesterol by 13%
claims.mechanism: Combined effect of nuts with soy protein, oats, barley and psyllium on cholesterol absorption and synthesis

claims.claim: Indo-Mediterranean diet with walnuts and almonds reduced non-fatal heart attacks by 50% and sudden cardiac death by 60%
claims.mechanism: Omega-3 and omega-6 fatty acids reduce inflammation and improve lipid profile

consumption.preparation: Raw or minimally processed; traditional use with dried fruits as dessert
consumption.reasoning: Epidemiological data on 5 servings/week showing 40-60% heart attack risk reduction

relationships.description: Both provide complementary unsaturated fatty acid profiles; PREDIMED tested both as Mediterranean diet supplements
Walnuts provide plant-based omega-3 (ALA) complementing marine omega-3 (EPA/DHA) from fish

ingredients.name: Fish (Sardines, Anchovies, and other oily fish)

claims.claim: Rich in omega-3 fatty acids that reduce inflammation
claims.mechanism: Omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids activate anti-inflammatory pathways (resolvins, protectins)

claims.claim: Replacing meat with fish contributes to lower cardiovascular mortality
claims.mechanism: Reduces saturated fat and L-carnitine/choline intake while increasing omega-3 fatty acids, lowering TMAO production and inflammation

consumption.amount: Regular consumption as part of Mediterranean diet
consumption.preparation: Home-cured (anchovies, sardines), preserved, or fresh; traditional preparations include bottarga and sardella
consumption.reasoning: Key protein source in traditional Mediterranean diet replacing red meat

relationships.description: Marine omega-3 (EPA/DHA) from fish complements plant-based omega-3 (ALA) from walnuts for comprehensive anti-inflammatory coverage
Combined unsaturated fat sources that together explain why Crete had low heart disease despite 40% calories from fat

claims.claim: Slow-fermented sourdough has superior nutritional qualities compared to white bread made with refined flour and baker's yeast
claims.mechanism: Lactobacillus bacteria in sourdough starter ferment in symbiosis with yeasts, breaking down phytic acid and improving mineral bioavailability; wholegrain flour retains wheat germ and bran providing fibre and micronutrients

claims.claim: High-fibre whole grains contribute to cholesterol lowering
claims.mechanism: Dietary fibre blocks reabsorption of cholesterol and bile acids in small intestine; low-glycaemic foods rich in fibre reduce circulating insulin and increase short-chain fatty acid production, both of which inhibit hepatic cholesterol synthesis

claims.claim: Contains ferulic acid which inhibits inflammation
claims.mechanism: Ferulic acid phytochemical found in whole grains inhibits inflammatory pathways

consumption.preparation: Made with freshly ground local durum wheat flour containing wheat germ and bran; slow-leavened with sourdough starter (lactobacillus + yeast symbiosis)
consumption.reasoning: Traditional Mediterranean staple; provides 30-45g daily fibre as part of overall diet

relationships.description: Together provide complementary amino acids and combined soluble/insoluble fibre for gut microbiome diversity
Traditional pairing; bread with EVOO provides slow-release carbohydrates with healthy fats

claims.claim: Contribute to high dietary fibre intake (30-45g/day) that lowers cholesterol
claims.mechanism: Water-soluble fibre blocks reabsorption of cholesterol and bile acids in small intestine; each additional gram of soluble fibre lowers LDL-cholesterol by ~1.12 mg/L

claims.claim: Promote beneficial gut bacteria that produce anti-inflammatory metabolites
claims.mechanism: Insoluble fibre increases Bacteroidetes and Firmicutes populations which produce short-chain fatty acids (butyrate, propionate) that bind to specific receptors to inhibit inflammation and increase regulatory T cells

claims.claim: Plant protein source that reduces TMAO production compared to animal protein
claims.mechanism: Lower choline and L-carnitine content (~50% less than Western diet) means less substrate for gut bacteria to produce TMAO, reducing cardiovascular disease risk by ~20%

consumption.amount: Regular servings as pasta sauce base
consumption.preparation: Cooked as spicy sauce served with homemade pasta (chickpeas, lentils, fava beans)
consumption.reasoning: Key plant protein and fibre source in traditional Mediterranean diet

relationships.description: Legumes + grains provide complete amino acid profiles; combined fibre feeds diverse gut bacteria populations
Together provide the high fibre intake (30-45g/day) that drives microbiome diversity and SCFA production

ingredients.name: Vegetables (leafy greens, wild herbs, diverse varieties)

claims.claim: Provide antioxidants that protect against oxidative damage driving chronic diseases
claims.mechanism: Vitamin C (~400mg/day), vitamin E (~17mg/day), beta-carotene (~6000mg equivalents/day), selenium (~120mg/day) neutralise free radicals and prevent LDL oxidation that drives atherosclerotic plaque formation

claims.claim: 53% reduced risk of Crohn's disease with higher consumption of vegetables and fruit
claims.mechanism: Vegetable fibre (psyllium, pectin, cellulose) reduces severity of colitis; fibre feeds beneficial gut bacteria producing anti-inflammatory short-chain fatty acids

claims.claim: Protective effect against heart disease from air pollutant exposure
claims.mechanism: Dietary antioxidants from vegetables neutralise oxidative damage caused by long-term pollutant exposure

claims.claim: High-fibre vegetable consumption increases regulatory T cells protecting against allergic and autoimmune disorders
claims.mechanism: Fibre fermentation produces butyrate and propionate which bind to receptors increasing regulatory T cell levels

consumption.amount: Large portions at every main meal; diverse types including wild herbs
consumption.preparation: Both raw (mixed salads with lettuce, dandelions, plantain, watercress, mallow, purslane) and cooked (chard, spinach, escarole stir-fried in olive oil with garlic); preserved (pickled eggplant, sun-dried tomatoes, mushrooms, zucchini, olives)
consumption.reasoning: No main meal was complete without lots of greens in traditional Mediterranean diet

relationships.description: EVOO enhances absorption of fat-soluble vitamins and antioxidants from vegetables; traditional dressing combination
Combined vegetable and legume fibre provides the 30-45g daily fibre that drives microbiome benefits

claims.claim: Part of cholesterol-lowering food portfolio reducing LDL-cholesterol by 13%
claims.mechanism: Water-soluble fibre blocks reabsorption of cholesterol and bile acids in small intestine

claims.claim: Reduces severity of colitis
claims.mechanism: Soluble fibre feeds beneficial gut bacteria, produces anti-inflammatory short-chain fatty acids; directly modulates intestinal immune response

claims.claim: Supplementation causes substantial improvement in gastrointestinal symptoms in ulcerative colitis patients in remission
claims.mechanism: Prebiotic fibre supports beneficial bacterial populations and SCFA production; reduces intestinal inflammation

consumption.preparation: As fibre supplement or incorporated in foods
consumption.reasoning: Specifically tested and shown to reduce colitis severity and improve GI symptoms

relationships.description: Both are soluble fibre sources in the cholesterol-lowering food portfolio
Different fibre types (soluble psyllium vs mixed legume fibre) feed complementary gut bacteria populations

claims.claim: Part of cholesterol-lowering food portfolio reducing LDL-cholesterol by 13%
claims.mechanism: Beta-glucan soluble fibre blocks reabsorption of cholesterol and bile acids in small intestine

consumption.amount: As part of varied whole grain intake
consumption.reasoning: Named specifically in cholesterol-lowering food trial

relationships.description: Both tested together in cholesterol-lowering food portfolio trial with complementary soluble fibre types
Combined soluble fibre sources for cholesterol reduction

claims.claim: Part of cholesterol-lowering food portfolio reducing LDL-cholesterol by 13%
claims.mechanism: Beta-glucan soluble fibre blocks reabsorption of cholesterol and bile acids in small intestine

consumption.amount: As part of varied whole grain intake
consumption.preparation: Used in traditional friselle bread (durum wheat and barley crispbread)
consumption.reasoning: Named in cholesterol-lowering trial; traditional Mediterranean grain

relationships.description: Both tested together in cholesterol-lowering food portfolio trial

claims.claim: Part of cholesterol-lowering food portfolio reducing LDL-cholesterol by 13%
claims.mechanism: Plant protein with isoflavones that modulate LDL receptor expression and cholesterol metabolism

consumption.amount: As part of plant protein intake
consumption.preparation: As soy protein or soybean oil
consumption.reasoning: Named in cholesterol-lowering trial; also mentioned in Indo-Mediterranean diet trial using soybean oil

relationships.description: Combined plant protein sources that together lower cholesterol through multiple mechanisms

claims.claim: Moderate consumption with meals is part of the traditional Mediterranean dietary pattern associated with lower cardiovascular mortality
claims.mechanism: Polyphenols (resveratrol, anthocyanins) provide antioxidant effects; consumed only with meals to moderate absorption

consumption.preparation: Consumed only with food, never independently
consumption.reasoning: Traditional pattern: 'a glass of red wine was consumed regularly but only with meals'

relationships.description: Together accounted for ~25% of calories in traditional Neapolitan diet surveyed by Keys

claims.claim: 53% reduced risk of Crohn's disease with higher consumption of vegetables and fruit
claims.mechanism: Pectin and other fruit fibres feed beneficial gut bacteria and produce anti-inflammatory short-chain fatty acids

claims.claim: Provide antioxidant vitamins (especially vitamin C) that protect against oxidative damage
claims.mechanism: Vitamin C neutralises free radicals, prevents LDL oxidation

consumption.amount: Seasonal fruits as dessert; dried fruits (peaches, nectarines, grapes, figs) in winter
consumption.preparation: Fresh seasonal or sun-dried; fig jam (no added sugar, figs' natural sweetness) for breakfast

relationships.description: Traditional pairing as dessert: dried fruits with nuts
Combined fruit and vegetable intake provides diverse phytochemicals and antioxidants

claims.claim: Each additional gram of water-soluble fibre lowers LDL-cholesterol by approximately 1.12 mg/L
claims.mechanism: Soluble fibre blocks reabsorption of cholesterol and bile acids in the small intestine

claims.claim: Low-glycaemic fibre-rich foods reduce circulating insulin and increase short-chain fatty acid production, both inhibiting hepatic cholesterol synthesis
claims.mechanism: SCFAs from fibre fermentation directly inhibit HMG-CoA reductase in liver; lower insulin reduces VLDL production

claims.claim: Insoluble fibre is the most important dietary influence on gut bacteria composition
claims.mechanism: Insoluble fibre selectively promotes growth of Bacteroidetes and Firmicutes that produce butyrate and propionate

claims.claim: Short-chain fatty acids from fibre fermentation inhibit inflammation and increase regulatory T cells
claims.mechanism: Butyrate and propionate bind to GPR43/GPR109A receptors on immune cells, suppressing NF-kB pathway and promoting Treg differentiation

claims.claim: Psyllium, pectin and cellulose fibre reduce severity of colitis; methylcellulose worsens it
claims.mechanism: Different fibre types are fermented differently by gut bacteria; natural fibres produce beneficial metabolites while synthetic methylcellulose does not

claims.claim: Greater risk of Crohn's recurrence in those who avoid fibre-rich foods
claims.mechanism: Fibre deprivation starves beneficial bacteria, reducing SCFA production and weakening intestinal barrier integrity

claims.claim: Decreased fibre consumption in Western diets may contribute to increase in asthma, type 1 diabetes, and multiple sclerosis
claims.mechanism: Reduced SCFA production leads to decreased regulatory T cells and impaired immune tolerance, promoting allergic and autoimmune disorders

consumption.preparation: From whole grains, legumes, vegetables, nuts and dried fruits - not supplements
consumption.reasoning: Mediterranean diet naturally provides 30-45g/day from diverse plant sources

relationships.description: Major fibre source in Mediterranean diet
Combined sources needed to reach 30-45g/day target
Whole grain bread major contributor to daily fibre intake

claims.claim: Rich in lycopene and vitamin C providing antioxidant protection
claims.mechanism: Lycopene is a potent carotenoid antioxidant; vitamin C neutralises free radicals

consumption.preparation: Fresh diced, as sauce, sun-dried preserved in EVOO; paired with garlic, basil, oregano
consumption.reasoning: Ubiquitous in traditional Mediterranean cuisine as described throughout chapter

relationships.description: Fat from EVOO enhances absorption of fat-soluble lycopene from tomatoes
Traditional pairing providing complementary phytochemical profiles

claims.claim: Provides phytochemicals with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties as part of Mediterranean diet pattern
claims.mechanism: Allicin and organosulfur compounds modulate inflammatory pathways

consumption.amount: Used as flavoring in most dishes
consumption.preparation: Fresh, in olive oil dressings, with cooked greens, in preserved vegetables
consumption.reasoning: Mentioned repeatedly as standard ingredient in grandmother's cooking

relationships.description: Traditional pairing in Mediterranean cooking

ingredients.name: Honey and Natural Sweeteners (mosto cotto, miele di fichi)

claims.claim: Traditional alternative to refined sugar for occasional sweets
claims.mechanism: Lower glycaemic impact than refined sugar; contains trace minerals and antioxidants absent in refined sugar

consumption.amount: Small amounts, only on special occasions
consumption.preparation: Honey, mosto cotto (wine grape juice reduced to dark syrup), miele di fichi (fig honey from cooked fig syrup)
consumption.reasoning: Traditional sweeteners used only for special occasion desserts, not daily consumption
